CHAPMAN GLOBAL MEDICAL CENTER D/P SNF

Overview

Address
2601 EAST CHAPMAN AVENUE, ORANGE, CA 92869
Phone
7146330011
Certified beds
27
Avg daily residents
24 (87% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1997-08-01
Setting
Urban
4 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 4/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 4/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 8.31 8.28 3.87 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 1.03 1.45 0.48 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 3.99 1.84 1.86 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 3.29 4.99 1.53 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 5.02 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.00 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
8.14
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
1.03
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.79
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
25.0%
annual
RN turnover
14.3%
annual
Administrators departed
New facility — insufficient data.

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
2.93
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
2.13
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-02-13 18 16 5 60 1 60
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-02-15 24 21 3 80 1 80
Total weighted health score
65.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (55)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

9 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
46 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-10-03 2025-11-15
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-05-02 2025-06-02
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. B 2025-02-13 2025-03-30
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2025-02-13 2025-03-30
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2025-02-13 2025-03-30
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-02-13 2025-03-30
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2025-02-13 2025-03-30
0694 Provide for the safe, appropriate administration of IV fluids for a resident when needed. D 2025-02-13 2025-03-30
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-02-13 2025-03-30
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2025-02-13 2025-03-30
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2025-02-13 2025-03-30
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-02-13 2025-03-30
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. D 2025-02-13 2025-03-30
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. B 2025-02-13 2025-03-30
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-02-13 2025-03-30
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. B 2025-02-13 2025-03-30
0838 Conduct and document a facility-wide assessment to determine what resources are necessary to care for residents competently during both day-to-day operations (including nights and weekends) and emergencies. D 2025-02-13 2025-03-30
0882 Designate a qualified infection preventionist to be responsible for the infection prevent and control program in the nursing home. D 2025-02-13 2025-03-30
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2024-09-25 2024-10-31
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. B 2024-02-15 2024-03-08
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. B 2024-02-15 2024-03-08
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. B 2024-02-15 2024-03-08
0604 Ensure that each resident is free from the use of physical restraints, unless needed for medical treatment. D 2024-02-15 2024-03-08
0635 Provide doctor's orders for the resident's immediate care at the time the resident was admitted. B 2024-02-15 2024-03-08
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-02-15 2024-03-08
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-02-15 2024-03-08
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2024-02-15 2024-03-08
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2024-02-15 2024-03-08
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2024-02-15 2024-03-08
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2024-02-15 2024-03-08
0694 Provide for the safe, appropriate administration of IV fluids for a resident when needed. D 2024-02-15 2024-03-08
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2024-02-15 2024-03-08
0700 Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. D 2024-02-15 2024-03-08
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2024-02-15 2024-03-08
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2024-02-15 2024-03-08
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. D 2024-02-15 2024-03-08
0813 Have a policy regarding use and storage of foods brought to residents by family and other visitors. D 2024-02-15 2024-03-08
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. B 2024-02-15 2024-03-08
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-02-15 2024-03-08
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. D 2024-02-15 2024-03-08
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. E 2023-09-19 2023-10-19
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. B 2023-08-31 2023-09-01
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2023-02-21 2023-03-21
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2023-02-21 2023-03-21
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2023-02-21 2023-03-21
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. E 2023-02-21 2023-03-21
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2023-02-21 2023-03-21
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2023-02-21 2023-03-21
0700 Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. E 2023-02-21 2023-03-21
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2023-02-21 2023-03-21

If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. 0700.description
Deficiencies 0700 — scope_severity D 0700.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0700 — scope_severity E 0700.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0700 — survey_date 2024-02-15 0700.survey_date
Deficiencies 0700 — survey_date 2023-02-21 0700.survey_date
Deficiencies 0700 — tag 0700 0700.tag
Deficiencies 0700 — tag 0700 0700.tag
Deficiencies 0755 — correction_date 2025-03-30 0755.correction_date
Deficiencies 0755 — description Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. 0755.description
Deficiencies 0755 — scope_severity D 0755.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0755 — survey_date 2025-02-13 0755.survey_date
Deficiencies 0755 — tag 0755 0755.tag
Deficiencies 0756 — correction_date 2025-03-30 0756.correction_date
Deficiencies 0756 — description Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. 0756.description
Deficiencies 0756 — scope_severity D 0756.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0756 — survey_date 2025-02-13 0756.survey_date
Deficiencies 0756 — tag 0756 0756.tag
Deficiencies 0758 — correction_date 2023-03-21 0758.correction_date
Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description
Deficiencies 0758 — scope_severity D 0758.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0758 — survey_date 2023-02-21 0758.survey_date
Deficiencies 0758 — tag 0758 0758.tag

Deficiencies 0759 — description Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. 0759.description

Deficiencies 0761 — description Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. 0761.description
